checks.wrongMoveCount: We expected the ball to move twice around the game area and land back in the same starting place.
checks.missedLeftWall: The ball didn't seem to touch the middle of the left hand side.
checks.missedRightWall: The ball didn't seem to touch the middle of the right hand side.
checks.missedTopMiddle: The ball didn't seem to touch the top middle.
checks.missedBottomMiddle: The ball didn't seem to touch the bottom middle.

bounceBall.name: Make the ball bounce around
bounceBall.description: Create a ball and make it bounce off all four walls of the game area.

addBall.name: Add the ball
addBall.description: Add the ball and make it bounce around the game area.

createBall.question: How do I get a ball to work with?
createBall.answer: Use `new` to make one: `let ball = new Ball()`. Then pass `ball` to `moveBall(ball)` inside the loop to make it move.

detectWall.question: How do I tell when the ball has reached a wall?
detectWall.answer: The ball's edge is `radius` away from its centre. For the left wall, check `ball.cx - ball.radius <= 0`. For the right wall, `ball.cx + ball.radius >= 100`.

reverseVelocity.question: What should I do when the ball hits a wall?
reverseVelocity.answer: Set the velocity so it moves the other way next step. At the left wall set `ball.xVelocity = 1`; at the right wall set it to `-1`. Use `=`, not `+=`, so the velocity stays exactly `1` or `-1`.

checkAllWalls.question: Why does my ball still escape sometimes?
checkAllWalls.answer: You probably only checked two walls. Add the same kind of check for the top (`ball.cy - ball.radius <= 0`) and bottom (`ball.cy + ball.radius >= 100`), flipping `yVelocity` instead of `xVelocity`.

describers.moveBall: Moves the ball according to its velocity

ball.description: Creates a new Ball instance with properties: `cx`, `cy` (center position), `radius`, `xVelocity`, and `yVelocity`.

moveBall.description: Moves the ball according to its current `xVelocity` and `yVelocity`.
